title: salix × salischii
intro: Salix × salischii, also known as the Salix × sepulcralis or Black Salicin Willow, is a hybrid deciduous shrub or small tree resulting from the cross between Salix alba (White Willow) and Salix fragilis (Crack Willow). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to produce salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. Consuming Salix × salischii has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating headaches and arthritis symptoms, and acting as an antiseptic agent to treat wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
